anti_claims: This organ JSON seed does not flip source authority away from core/organ_atlas.json and core/organ_registry.json.
Required paper/mechanism/code links prove declared structural references only, not runtime correctness or release readiness.
Selective concept, principle, axiom, and sibling-organ edges are not inferred from prose, specialty, family, or evidence class.
Absent selective organ edges are residual pressure, not evidence that no neighbours exist.
Generated markdown, graph, health, and atlas projections cannot be read back as source evidence.

omitted: runtime outputs beyond named public receipts
selective doctrine neighbours not named by atlas source rows
omission_receipt.reason: Organ instance preserves public-safe atlas/registry fields. Required links resolve only when source ids or paths resolve; selective law/concept/principle edges remain residual unless source evidence names ids.

residual_pressure.reentry_condition: Populate missing paper/mechanism/code links from source evidence, then bind concept/principle/axiom/wires_to edges only when ids are named.

organ_payload.claim_ceiling: It validates the shape and presence of synthetic replay metadata and receipt references only - it does not run any experiment, metric script, or rerun, does not authorize any claim that a paper was actually replicated, that a benchmark score was achieved, or that the underlying science is correct, and it never calls providers, exposes private paper/data bodies, or authorizes publication or release.

source_atlas_row.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to validate a replication-claim bundle: that the two synthetic paper capsules each carry all fourteen required evidence references (rubric tree, contribution decomposition, allowed-input refs, scaffold, experiment DAG, metric scripts, declared+actual artifact-hash refs, grader report, budget, ablation diff, failure taxonomy, cold-rerun receipt), that the policy forbids the prohibited shortcuts, and that all eight negative fixtures fire their expected error codes (author-code reuse, hidden-rubric leakage, report-only success, benchmark overclaim, private-body leak, unbounded compute, final-answer-only grading, undeclared artifact hash). It validates reference presence and receipt shape only; it does not execute the scripts, run a rerun, or call providers.
source_atlas_row.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates the shape and presence of synthetic replay metadata and receipt references only - it does not run any experiment, metric script, or rerun, does not authorize any claim that a paper was actually replicated, that a benchmark score was achieved, or that the underlying science is correct, and it never calls providers, exposes private paper/data bodies, or authorizes publication or release.

code_loci.role: Runtime authority for research-replication fixture validation, exported-bundle validation, source-module manifest checks, public trace construction, negative-case enforcement, authority ceilings, and body-free receipt writing.

source_atlas_row.human_gloss: It checks whether a claim that an AI agent "replicated a research paper" comes with the paper trail real replication would leave behind. It re-runs nothing; instead it confirms the bundle names every required piece of evidence: a breakdown of the paper's contributions, a grading rubric, the list of allowed public inputs, a from-scratch repo scaffold, an experiment plan, the metric scripts, a roster of declared file-hashes for the outputs plus hashes that all stay inside that roster, a grader report, a capped compute/runtime budget, an ablation diff, a failure list, and a cold-rerun receipt. It also catches eight ways a claim can cheat: reusing the original authors' code, leaking a hidden rubric, calling a run a "success" when only a write-up backs it, asserting a benchmark score, leaking a private paper or dataset body, using unbounded compute, grading only the final answer, or pointing at a file-hash that was never declared. The work runs on two made-up sample papers (one machine-learning method, one computational-science study), and the generated receipt shows which of the eight cheats each test case triggered, rather than taking "it was replicated" on trust.

mechanism_refs.edge_justification: The organ's source module, exported research-replication bundle, fixtures, acceptance/runtime receipts, source-module manifest, and mechanism row validate bounded public research-replication claim-shape evidence without running experiments, asserting benchmark success, exporting private bodies, or authorizing publication.

source_atlas_row.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret-exclusion scan, receipts, schemas, and the shared public-agent-execution-trace macro tool); does not invoke another named organ at runtime. Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.

source_registry_row.claim_ceiling: projection mechanics only, not domain correctness
source_registry_row.classification_basis: public agent-execution trace refactor builds replay spans, digests, and coverage checks from declared artifact, rubric, budget, failure-taxonomy, and cold-rerun refs
